title: 10 best herbal teas for laryngitis

teas.tea_desc: Slippery elm tea provides relief for laryngitis due to its demulcent property, meaning it has a softening and soothing effect. This tree's inner bark contains mucilage, a substance that becomes a slick gel when mixed with water. By coating the throat's lining, this gel provides a barrier against irritants and reduces inflammation. Furthermore, it alleviates pain and irritation while promoting healing in the affected tissues. Slippery elm tea stands out as a time-honored natural remedy for laryngitis, approved by the German Commission E for treating inflammation of the mouth and throat.
tea_parts: Bark: The slippery elm's inner bark is loaded with mucilage, a substance that becomes a slick gel when mixed with water. This gel coats and soothes inflamed mucous membranes, making it perfect for laryngitis relief.
Leaves: Slippery elm leaves are also rich in mucilage. When steeped in hot water, they release this soothing substance, providing a natural remedy for laryngitis-related irritation.

Seeds: While not as mucilaginous as the bark or leaves, slippery elm seeds still contain some of this beneficial slimy substance. They can be used to make tea and assist in alleviating laryngitis symptoms.

Roots: Although less commonly used than the bark or leaves, slippery elm roots can also be employed to make medicinal tea. They contain mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect inflamed tissues in the throat.
tea_recipe: Measure: Scoop 1 tablespoon of slippery elm bark powder into a tea infuser or teapot with a strainer.
Boil: Pour 8 ounces of water that has reached a rolling boil over the slippery elm bark.
Steep: Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to release the medicinal properties.
Stir: Mix in honey or lemon juice to enhance the taste and soothing effect, if desired.
Serve: Pour the tea into a cup and drink it while warm, up to three times a day for laryngitis relief.
tea_constituents: Mucilage: The demulcent properties of mucilage help soothe irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the larynx.
Tannins: Astringent tannins help reduce inflammation and swelling in the larynx, alleviating symptoms of laryngitis.
Flavonoids: Anti-inflammatory flavonoids found in slippery elm may help decrease inflammation in the larynx, promoting healing.
Starch: Slippery elm tea contains starch, which can help coat and protect the lining of the throat, providing relief from irritation.
Gallic acid: This antioxidant can help reduce oxidative stress in the throat, protecting the larynx and supporting overall healing.
Kaempferol: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it helpful for soothing and healing the larynx during laryngitis.
Quercetin: As an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory, quercetin can help address the underlying causes of laryngitis, relieving symptoms.

teas.tea_desc: Marshmallow tea brings immediate relief to laryngitis due to the demulcent property of its main ingredient, Althaea officinalis. This plant-based substance creates a protective film over the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and inflammation. As a result, soreness in the throat significantly decreases, and the natural hydrating effect of marshmallow tea soothes dryness. Furthermore, it promotes expectoration of phlegm, enabling clearer breathing and improved vocal cord function. Ultimately, marshmallow tea's anti-inflammatory and healing qualities address the discomfort and symptoms of laryngitis effectively.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, marshmallow leaves soothe irritated throat tissues due to laryngitis.
Flowers: Abundant in mucilage, marshmallow flowers coat and protect inflamed mucous membranes, providing relief from laryngitis.
Roots: High in antioxidants, marshmallow roots boost the immune system, helping the body fight laryngitis.
Bark: Containing healing polysaccharides, marshmallow bark forms a protective layer over throat tissues, alleviating symptoms of laryngitis.
tea_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water, 2 tablespoons of dried marshmallow root, and 1 teaspoon of grated ginger; bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove solids, then stir in 1 tablespoon of honey and the juice of half a lemon.
Warm up the tea again, but don't let it boil.
Pour the tea into a mug and optionally garnish with a lemon slice.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to coat your throat, and drink up to 3 times a day as needed.
tea_constituents: Mucilage: The mucilage in marshmallow tea coats and soothes irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from laryngitis.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the throat, alleviating laryngitis.
Ascorbic acid: Ascorbic acid can boost the immune system, helping the body fight off infections that may cause laryngitis.
Tannins: Tannins have astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, providing relief from laryngitis.
Pectin: Pectin, like mucilage, forms a protective film over the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and easing symptoms of laryngitis.

teas.tea_desc: Licorice tea brings immediate relief to laryngitis due to its demulcent properties, which involve a film-forming ability that coats the throat's mucous membrane. This tea's primary ingredient, glycyrrhizin, exhibits anti-inflammatory qualities, reducing swelling in the throat and alleviating discomfort. Furthermore, licorice tea soothes irritation by increasing the mucus's production, ensuring a protective lubricant layer over the affected area. Additionally, this natural remedy encourages the removal of phlegm, making it easier to expel mucus and other foreign particles. Lastly, the warm temperature of licorice tea increases blood circulation in the throat, enhancing the immune system's response to inflammation and infection.
tea_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, the roots of the licorice plant soothe inflamed mucous membranes, providing relief for laryngitis.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and volatile oils, licorice leaves promote respiratory health, easing laryngitis symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice plant are a natural source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, benefiting those suffering from laryngitis.
Seeds: Loaded with beneficial phytochemicals, licorice seeds support a healthy immune response, alleviating laryngitis symptoms.
tea_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root pieces.
Optional: Add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for laryngitis relief.
tea_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This compound is the primary active component in licorice root and has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and soothe irritation in the throat caused by laryngitis.
Flavonoids: These natural compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to protect the throat from damage and reduce inflammation caused by laryngitis.
Coumarins: These compounds found in licorice root have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to alleviate pain and inflammation in the throat caused by laryngitis.

Triterpenoids: These compounds have been shown to have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to thin and expel mucus from the respiratory tract and reduce inflammation caused by laryngitis.

Volatile oils: These oils found in licorice root, such as anethole and licochalcone, have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to fight off infections and reduce inflammation caused by laryngitis.

teas.tea_desc: Chamomile tea brings relief to laryngitis due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling in the throat. This herbal tea's soothing warmth eases discomfort by calming irritation. Chamomile's rich antioxidant content strengthens the immune system, helping the body fight laryngitis-causing infections. Furthermore, chamomile tea's mild sedative effects promote restful sleep, aiding in recovery. Its mucolytic action thins and loosens mucus, making it easier to expel.
tea_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, chamomile flowers produce a soothing tea that helps alleviate irritation in the throat, a common symptom of laryngitis.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, chamomile leaves create a comforting tea that helps reduce inflammation in the larynx, providing relief from laryngitis.
Seeds: Packed with anti-inflammatory properties, chamomile seeds produce a potent tea beneficial for treating laryngitis and soothing the related discomfort.
tea_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and let 1 chamomile tea bag steep for 5-7 minutes.
Optionally - Add a slice of lemon and a drizzle of honey to taste for additional soothing properties.
Stir - Mix all ingredients together until honey is fully dissolved.
Wait - Let the tea cool down to a warm temperature that is comfortable to drink.
Sip - Drink the tea slowly, holding it in the back of your mouth and throat for a few seconds before swallowing.
tea_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the larynx and soothe irritation in the throat.
Chamazulene: This compound has been found to have antiphlogistic and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the larynx caused by laryngitis.
Bisabolol: This terpene has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and analgesic properties, making it effective in soothing irritation and reducing inflammation in the larynx.
Matricin: This sesquiterpene lactone is converted to chamazulene during steam distillation, and has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate symptoms of laryngitis.
Spiroether: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling in the larynx and soothe irritation in the throat.

teas.tea_desc: Peppermint tea brings immediate relief to laryngitis due to its active component, menthol, which possesses strong antiviral and antibacterial properties. This component works by forming a soothing barrier over the larynx, reducing inflammation and irritation. Moreover, peppermint tea stimulates blood flow, accelerating the healing process. Its natural anti-inflammatory agents further alleviate pain and swelling in the throat. Additionally, peppermint tea liquefies mucus, making it easier to expel, and inhibits the growth of harmful microorganisms causing the infection.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in menthol, the leaves offer effective cough suppressant and analgesic properties, soothing the inflamed larynx.
Flowers: Containing rosmarinic acid, flowers provide strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping alleviate laryngitis symptoms.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, seeds offer potent antimicrobial properties, aiding in fighting infections causing laryngitis.
tea_recipe: In a teapot, add boiling water to 1 teaspoon of crushed dried peppermint leaves.
Let the mixture steep for 10 minutes to allow the peppermint oils to infuse.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, pouring it into a cup.
Optionally, add honey to taste for soothing a sore throat.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day, to help alleviate laryngitis symptoms.
tea_constituents: Menthol: Acts as a local anesthetic and analgesic, helping to relieve pain and inflammation in the larynx.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with laryngitis.
Melissa officinalis L.: Has antiviral and antimicrobial effects, helping to fight off viral or bacterial infections causing laryngitis.
Flavanones: Possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to soothe irritated mucous membranes and alleviate coughing.
Tannins: Provide astringent effects, reducing swelling and promoting healing of the laryngeal tissues.

teas.tea_desc: Thyme tea provides relief for laryngitis due to its well-documented antibacterial and antiviral properties, derived from its active compounds, namely thymol and carvacrol. These compounds actively combat infection-causing agents, reducing inflammation and soothing irritation in the throat. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties help loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and clear the airways. Furthermore, thyme tea's antioxidant qualities strengthen the immune system, promoting faster recovery. Consequently, these properties position thyme tea as an effective natural remedy for laryngitis symptoms, providing both soothing relief and combating the root cause of the ailment.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, leaves of thyme are commonly used to soothe irritation and reduce inflammation in the throat associated with laryngitis.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, thyme flowers help protect the throat tissue from damage, supporting quicker recovery from laryngitis.
Stems: Containing essential oils, thyme stems offer antibacterial properties, aiding in eliminating the cause of laryngitis and promoting faster healing.
tea_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves or 2 teaspoons of fresh thyme to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the thyme leaves.
Sip the warm thyme tea, allowing it to soothe your laryngitis for at least 15-20 minutes.
tea_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antiseptic and antibacterial properties, helping to kill off any harmful bacteria that may be causing the laryngitis.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol also has antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and fight infection in the larynx.
Apigenin: Apigenin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe irritation and reduce swelling in the larynx.
Naringenin: Naringenin has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the larynx from further damage and promote healing.
Luteolin: Luteolin has been shown to have expectorant properties, which can help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving symptoms of laryngitis.

teas.tea_desc: Sage tea provides relief for laryngitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which reduce swelling in the throat and alleviate coughing spasms. The rosmarinic acid in sage tea inhibits the production of substances that cause inflammation, while its soothing effect coats the throat, providing immediate comfort. Furthermore, sage tea's antibacterial properties help fight off any potential infection contributing to laryngitis, promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, sage tea's astringent qualities help tighten and tone throat tissues, reducing irritation and discomfort.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, leaves help soothe inflamed tissues in the throat.
Flowers: High in antioxidants, flowers aid in reducing irritation and promoting healing.
Aerial parts: While not a specific part, it's worth noting that aerial parts of some plants have medicinal value; however, they are excluded from this list.
Stems: Containing essential oils, stems help alleviate congestion and ease breathing.
Roots: Packed with therapeutic properties, roots provide relief from pain and reduce inflammation caused by laryngitis.
tea_recipe: In a small pot, bring 2 cups of water to a boil, then add 2 teaspoons of dried sage leaves.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the sage leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Sip the sage tea, aiming for a total consumption of 1-2 cups per day.
tea_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the throat and alleviate the symptoms of laryngitis.
Carnosic Acid: Carnosic Acid has antioxidant properties that can help protect the throat from damage and support the healing process.
Ursolic Acid: This compound has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help clear mucus and reduce inflammation in the throat.
Camphor: Camphor has a cooling and numbing effect, which can help soothe a sore throat and alleviate pain associated with laryngitis.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol has expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help clear mucus and reduce inflammation in the throat.

teas.tea_desc: Echinacea tea contains active compounds, such as alkamides and caffeic acid, that possess anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. These properties help reduce swelling in the larynx, thereby alleviating the pain and discomfort associated with laryngitis. Echinacea also has antiviral and antibacterial effects, which aid in fighting off infections causing laryngitis. Furthermore, it stimulates the production of white blood cells, enhancing the body's ability to combat illness. Lastly, echinacea tea soothes irritated throats by providing a warm and comforting sensation, while its mild analgesic qualities help minimize the need for over-the-counter pain relievers.
tea_parts: Roots: The roots of the echinacea plant contain beneficial compounds that help bolster the immune system, making it effective in soothing laryngitis symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the echinacea plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce irritation in the throat, providing relief from laryngitis.
Leaves: The leaves of the echinacea plant are rich in antioxidants that fight off infections, making them useful for treating laryngitis.
Buds: The buds of the echinacea plant contain high levels of therapeutic compounds that can alleviate soreness and irritation in the throat.
Bark: (not a part of the echinacea plant used in medicinal teas)
tea_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea flowers or 4 tablespoons of fresh echinacea flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the echinacea flowers, discarding the solids.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Serve the tea while hot, up to three times a day, for laryngitis relief.
tea_constituents: Echinacea polysaccharides: These complex sugars found in echinacea can help stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms of laryngitis.
Echinacoside: A phenolic compound, echinacoside has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, helping to soothe irritation and potentially combat viral infections causing laryngitis.

Cichoric acid: An organic acid, cichoric acid exhibits antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects, which may help protect the throat from damage and bolster overall immune function during laryngitis.

Essential oils: Compounds such as caryophyllene, humulene, and germacrene found in echinacea's essential oils have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, potentially aiding in pain relief and reducing swelling in the larynx.
Alkamides: These lipophilic compounds display immune-enhancing, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic activities, which can help alleviate pain and inflammation, common symptoms of laryngitis.

teas.tea_desc: Goldenseal tea helps with laryngitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. This herbal tea contains berberine, a compound known to reduce inflammation in the mucous membranes, including the larynx. Moreover, goldenseal tea's antibacterial qualities help fight off infections causing laryngitis, thus promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, this tea soothes sore throats by creating a protective coating on the mucous membranes. Lastly, goldenseal tea boosts the immune system, increasing the body's ability to combat the laryngitis-causing ailment.
tea_parts: Roots: Goldenseal roots contain berberine, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe irritated throat tissues.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of goldenseal plants are rich in alkaloids, offering antibacterial and immune-boosting benefits, which aid in fighting laryngitis-causing infections.
Leaves: Goldenseal leaves have anticatarrhal actions, helping to reduce excess mucus production and alleviating the symptoms of laryngitis.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, goldenseal flowers provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, supporting overall throat health during laryngitis.
tea_recipe: In a saucepan, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried goldenseal root, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove goldenseal pieces, discarding the solid remnants.
Let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature, typically 5-10 minutes.
Sip the goldenseal tea slowly, aiming to finish 2-3 cups throughout the day.
tea_constituents: Berberine: Has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and fight off infection in the larynx.
Hydrastine: A bitter alkaloid that helps stimulate mucous membranes, increasing lubrication and soothing irritation in the throat.
Canadine: An isoquinoline alkaloid with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of laryngitis.
L-reuteri: A probiotic that can help maintain a healthy balance of bacteria in the mouth and throat, potentially reducing the risk of laryngitis.
Rutin: A flavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to protect and heal damaged throat tissue.

teas.tea_desc: Turmeric tea provides relief from laryngitis due to its active ingredient, curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound works by inhibiting the production of inflammation-causing molecules, thus reducing swelling in the throat. Furthermore, curcumin's antibacterial qualities combat infection, a common cause of laryngitis. Turmeric tea also soothes irritation, creating a protective barrier in the throat. Additionally, this warming beverage helps loosen mucus, promoting its elimination and alleviating congestion.
tea_parts: Rhizomes: These are the horizontal underground stems of the turmeric plant that are rich in curcumin, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in soothing irritation in the throat caused by laryngitis.

Leaves: The leaves of the turmeric plant contain therapeutic compounds that have been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory ailments, including laryngitis, due to its expectorant and soothing properties.

Roots: The turmeric roots, or tubers, are used in medicinal teas for laryngitis because of their potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the throat.

Flowers: The flowers of the turmeric plant contain bioactive compounds that have been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory infections and reduce inflammation, making it a suitable addition to medicinal teas for laryngitis.
tea_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 4 cups of water and 1 teaspoon of grated fresh turmeric (or 1/2 teaspoon of dried turmeric powder).
Bring the mixture to a boil, then lower the heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a mug, pressing on the turmeric solids to extract all liquid.
Stir in honey and lemon juice to taste (optional, but recommended for soothing a sore throat).
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day as needed.
tea_constituents: Curcumin: Has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the throat caused by laryngitis.
Demethoxycurcumin: Has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate symptoms of laryngitis.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin: Has antioxidant properties that can help protect the throat from damage and support healing.
Turmerone: Has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that can help soothe a sore throat and expel mucus.
Atlantone: Has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off infections causing laryngitis.

other_remedies: Laryngitis, an inflammation of the larynx, often causes a hoarse or weak voice and, in severe cases, can result in a complete loss of voice. Two of the most common and effective natural remedies for laryngitis are hydration and rest. Staying hydrated helps keep the vocal cords lubricated, reducing irritation and inflammation. Aim to drink at least eight glasses of water a day, and avoid caffeine, alcohol, and sugary drinks, which can dehydrate the body. Resting the voice is crucial for healing, so avoid talking, whispering, or straining the vocal cords. Additionally, using a humidifier can help keep the air moist, easing throat discomfort. Finally, steam inhalation can provide relief by loosening mucus and reducing swelling in the throat. Incorporating these natural remedies into your routine can help alleviate the symptoms of laryngitis and promote faster healing.
causes: Viral Infections: Viral infections, such as the common cold or flu, inflame the vocal cords and surrounding tissues, leading to laryngitis. The virus attacks the mucous membranes of the larynx, causing inflammation and swelling.

Bacterial Infections: Bacterial infections, such as strep throat, infect the vocal cords and surrounding tissues, causing inflammation and swelling. This leads to irritation and inflammation of the larynx, resulting in laryngitis.
Acid Reflux: Stomach acid flows up into the throat, irritating the vocal cords and causing inflammation. This repetitive irritation leads to chronic inflammation and swelling, causing laryngitis.

Voice Strain: Prolonged or excessive use of the voice, such as screaming, shouting, or singing, puts strain on the vocal cords, causing fatigue and irritation. This leads to inflammation and swelling, resulting in laryngitis.
Allergies: Allergens, such as pollen or dust, irritate the vocal cords and surrounding tissues, causing inflammation and swelling. This leads to laryngitis and vocal cord irritation.
Inhaling Irritants: Inhaling pollutants, chemicals, or smoke irritates the vocal cords and surrounding tissues, causing inflammation and swelling. This leads to laryngitis and vocal cord damage.
Hormonal Changes: Hormonal fluctuations during pregnancy, menstruation, or menopause affect the vocal cords and laryngeal tissues, leading to inflammation and swelling, resulting in laryngitis.

Neurological Disorders: Certain neurological disorders, such as Parkinson's disease or multiple sclerosis, affect the nerves controlling the larynx, leading to muscle weakness and inflammation, resulting in laryngitis.
intro: Soothing herbal teas offer a gentle approach to alleviating laryngitis symptoms. Slippery elm tea, with its moisturizing properties, helps calm an inflamed throat, reducing discomfort and promoting healing. Throat Coat tea, a blend of herbs including slippery elm, licorice root, and marshmallow root, provides a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, allowing the throat to heal. Moreover, Ginger tea, with its natural anti-inflammatory properties, helps reduce swelling and ease pain. Additionally, Lemon and Honey tea, with its antibacterial properties, helps combat infection and soothe an irritated throat, promoting a speedy recovery.
definition: Laryngitis is a medical condition that causes inflammation of the larynx, also known as the voice box. This results in a hoarse or weak voice, and in severe cases, complete loss of voice. Laryngitis can impact people's lives significantly, interfering with their ability to communicate effectively, perform their jobs, and engage in social activities. It can also cause discomfort, pain, and difficulty swallowing, further impacting a person's quality of life. Children and professional voice users, such as singers and teachers, are particularly vulnerable to the effects of laryngitis.
